suggested_code_alias_crosswalk.BSPRN: No direct program_history code; likely related historical code is BSRN

part_a_program_guide_alignment.judgment_rationale: Program code identity is strong for most guides (110/115 direct matches), and title/version alignment is mostly good. A small but important alias/missing-code set must be handled explicitly before treating program_history as a strict one-to-one anchor.

part_b_roster_extractability.why: Per-guide AoS/SP rosters and context fields are already parsable and stable; attachability classing can be precomputed even when final disambiguation is deferred.

part_c_prep_artifact_recommendation.record_shape: one guide record containing roster rows (AoS and SP), plus per-guide alignment metadata and anchorability classes
core_fields: alignment_flags (program_history match class, title match class, version match class)
roster_rows[] with surface (aos/sp), guide_title_raw, guide_title_normalized, aos_group, sp_term, sp_cus, anchor_class, canonical_candidate_codes

source_of_fields.guides: parsed guide artifacts + corpus manifest metadata

part_c_prep_artifact_recommendation.expected_benefit_for_next_session: Material. Next extraction can focus on enrichment payload decisions on top of precomputed rosters/anchorability, instead of repeatedly recomputing identity alignment and title match classes.

direct_answers.is_program_history_good_anchor: Yes, usable with caveats (direct for 110/115 guides; requires alias crosswalk for 5 missing and handling of title/version drift cases).
direct_answers.can_prebuild_per_guide_rosters: Yes. Existing parsed artifacts support stable AoS/SP roster extraction with contextual fields and precomputed anchorability classes.
direct_answers.will_it_materially_help_next_session: Yes. It should reduce repeated matching/alignment work and make the larger enrichment pass primarily a policy+selection task.
main_unresolved_after_bridge: Cert/prereq signal noise and claim-safety gating
